samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.
I'll use the luminosity configure skill to read that value.

`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`**.

Configuration in this project (Lantern) is a tree of dotted `section.key` values resolved across two Tiers:

- **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone.
- **personal** — local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.

A plain `get` (no `--level`) resolves personal-over-team; here nothing overrides it at the personal Tier, so the team value `team-v` came through. A plain `set` with no `--level` writes to personal by default, so accidental changes never land in shared state.

Separate from these CLI-owned values, each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project/skill context into prompts — but that's user-edited directly, not something I read or write through `luminosity config`.

attachments.45f4ff115162f67c16d7debd6d7b9352:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`**.

Configuration in this project (Lantern) is a tree of dotted `section.key` values resolved across two Tiers:

- **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone.
- **personal** — local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.

A plain `get` (no `--level`) resolves personal-over-team; here nothing overrides it at the personal Tier, so the team value `team-v` came through. A plain `set` with no `--level` writes to personal by default, so accidental changes never land in shared state.

Separate from these CLI-owned values, each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project/skill context into prompts — but that's user-edited directly, not something I read or write through `luminosity config`.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.
I'll use the luminosity:configure skill to read that value.

arguments.description: Read core.example config value via luminosity CLI

messages.content:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`**.

Configuration in this project (called **Beacon**) works as a two-level, dotted `section.key` tree:

- **team** — a shared, committed file everyone sees.
- **personal** — a local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.

Reads with no level specified resolve personal-over-team; writes with no level go to personal by default, so changes never accidentally land in shared state. Here, `core.example` had no personal override, so the team value (`team-v`) was returned.

Beyond CLI-owned values, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt, plus optional per-skill context/instructions files under `.luminosity/skills/<name>/`.

attachments.5b83dd91e9176dc6ccfc3167d626f98b:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`**.

Configuration in this project (called **Beacon**) works as a two-level, dotted `section.key` tree:

- **team** — a shared, committed file everyone sees.
- **personal** — a local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.

Reads with no level specified resolve personal-over-team; writes with no level go to personal by default, so changes never accidentally land in shared state. Here, `core.example` had no personal override, so the team value (`team-v`) was returned.

Beyond CLI-owned values, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt, plus optional per-skill context/instructions files under `.luminosity/skills/<name>/`.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`** (personal-over-team resolution; presumably no personal-level override is set, so the team value wins).

How configuration works in this project (Lantern):

- **Two tiers**: a shared, committed **team** file and a local, git-ignored **personal** file. Personal overrides team for the same key.
- **Dotted keys**: values live at `section.key` paths (e.g. `core.example`).
- **Resolution**: a plain `get`/`set` with no `--level` resolves/writes personal-over-team — reads check personal first then fall back to team; writes default to personal so accidental changes never land in shared state.
- **CLI-owned**: all reads/writes go through `luminosity config get|set`, never direct file edits — the CLI owns file paths, frontmatter format, and precedence.
- **Free-form context**: separate from these values, each config file also carries a Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` personal) that's injected as project context into every skill's prompt — combined team-first, and edited directly by the user rather than via the CLI.
- **Skill-scoped context/instructions** work the same way but live under `.luminosity/skills/<skill-name>/` and are injected only into that skill's prompt.

attachments.20ed50709b48790a6ef0b826d6696e6e:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`** (personal-over-team resolution; presumably no personal-level override is set, so the team value wins).

How configuration works in this project (Lantern):

- **Two tiers**: a shared, committed **team** file and a local, git-ignored **personal** file. Personal overrides team for the same key.
- **Dotted keys**: values live at `section.key` paths (e.g. `core.example`).
- **Resolution**: a plain `get`/`set` with no `--level` resolves/writes personal-over-team — reads check personal first then fall back to team; writes default to personal so accidental changes never land in shared state.
- **CLI-owned**: all reads/writes go through `luminosity config get|set`, never direct file edits — the CLI owns file paths, frontmatter format, and precedence.
- **Free-form context**: separate from these values, each config file also carries a Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` personal) that's injected as project context into every skill's prompt — combined team-first, and edited directly by the user rather than via the CLI.
- **Skill-scoped context/instructions** work the same way but live under `.luminosity/skills/<skill-name>/` and are injected only into that skill's prompt.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

arguments.description: Read core.example config value via luminosity CLI

messages.content:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`** (personal-over-team resolution; no personal override is set for that key, so it's coming from the team file).

How configuration works in this project (Beacon):

- Values are dotted `section.key` pairs, resolved across two levels:
  - **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone.
  - **personal** — local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.
- A `get` with no `--level` resolves personal-over-team (which is what just happened here); a `set` with no `--level` writes to personal, so accidental changes never land in shared state.
- Separately from these CLI-owned values, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — team and personal bodies combine, team-first. Context/instructions can also be scoped to a single skill via `.luminosity/skills/<name>/context.md` and `instructions.md` (plus `.local.md` personal variants).
- All of this is managed exclusively through the `luminosity config`/`context`/`instructions` CLI — I never read or write the underlying files directly.

attachments.0bafb65ab720bf7dd691c73efdf6d24b:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`** (personal-over-team resolution; no personal override is set for that key, so it's coming from the team file).

How configuration works in this project (Beacon):

- Values are dotted `section.key` pairs, resolved across two levels:
  - **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone.
  - **personal** — local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.
- A `get` with no `--level` resolves personal-over-team (which is what just happened here); a `set` with no `--level` writes to personal, so accidental changes never land in shared state.
- Separately from these CLI-owned values, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — team and personal bodies combine, team-first. Context/instructions can also be scoped to a single skill via `.luminosity/skills/<name>/context.md` and `instructions.md` (plus `.local.md` personal variants).

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`** (from the shared team config, no personal override present).

**How configuration works here (Lantern):**
- Values live as dotted `section.key` entries across two **tiers**: **team** (shared, committed file) and **personal** (local, git-ignored) — personal overrides team.
- Reads/writes go only through the `luminosity config` CLI; the file paths, frontmatter format, and precedence are CLI-owned, never edited directly.
- Each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body (below the frontmatter) that gets injected as project/skill context into every skill's prompt — team-first, personal appended after. Editing that body is a manual, user-driven edit to `.luminosity/config.md` / `.luminosity/config.local.md`, not something done via the CLI.
